notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — not currently recordable for pi (see assessment.json, recipe applicable:false). This is the arc a quota-exhaustion episode WOULD produce IF pi shipped a self-enforced budget cap OR surfaced a distinct quota signal in its transcript. Modeled as: a happy-path turn (baseline) followed by a second turn that hits the cap and resolves to ready without sticking in working and without ever entering waiting. Anchored on working transitions to dodge pi's born-ready proc-<PID> placeholder. pi cannot self-abort at a token budget (no cap flag, pi 0.70.0) and pi-ai auto-retries provider 429s transparently with no distinct transcript line, so this target cannot be elicited live without faking transcript content.

text: Prompt 1 drives ready → working (happy-path baseline turn)
Prompt 1 completes cleanly back to ready (the baseline before the cap is hit)
Prompt 2 drives ready → working; this is the turn that hits the token/quota cap

Spec: agent hits its budget, emits a turn-end signal, and the session resolves working → ready without sticking and without ever entering waiting (no permission gate). Subsequent prompts staying ready until the budget resets is future work (would need a 3+ prompt recipe).
